event_text: Count Roger I of Sicily captures Malta from Muslim rule.
Malta becomes part of the Hohenstaufen Kingdom of Sicily.
The Sicilian Vespers changes the political setting of Malta within the Sicilian realm.
Maltese forces resist a Hafsid raid after the Battle of Malta.
Charles V grants Malta to the Order of St John.
The Great Siege of Malta ends with victory for the Knights of St John.
Valletta is founded after the Great Siege.
The Order of St John moves its convent to Valletta.
A plague outbreak strikes Malta.
Construction begins on the Floriana Lines to strengthen Valletta’s defences.
A major plague epidemic begins in Malta.
Napoleon captures Malta on his way to Egypt.
The Maltese uprising against French rule begins, leading to blockade and eventual French surrender.
Malta becomes a British protectorate after the French surrender.
The Treaty of Paris confirms Malta as a British colony.
The Council of Government is established.
Malta receives a partly elected Council of Government.
The opening of the Suez Canal increases Malta’s strategic importance.
The Malta Railway opens between Valletta and Mdina.
The Sette Giugno riots occur in Valletta.
Malta receives self-government under a new constitution.
The first Axis air raid on Malta begins a period of heavy bombing during the Second World War.
King George VI awards the George Cross to Malta.
Malta receives a new constitution and restored self-government.
A referendum supports integration with the United Kingdom but the plan is not implemented.
Malta becomes independent from Britain.
Malta becomes a republic.
British military forces leave Malta on Freedom Day.
Constitutional amendments strengthen parliamentary electoral rules.
Malta applies to join the European Communities.
Malta votes to join the European Union.
Malta joins the European Union.
Malta adopts the euro.
Malta votes to allow divorce.
Journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia is assassinated.
The COVID-19 pandemic affects Malta.
